strengths: Incorporation of client feedback at multiple stages highlights strong collaboration and communication ATL skills.
Detailed account of measurement taking and fabric cutting demonstrates effective self-management and precision.
Well-documented sewing and embellishment sequence reflects strong organizational skills.
Comprehensive research plan with a clear CARRDS evaluation shows critical thinking and source evaluation.
Adaptive problem-solving when falling behind (brainstorming simpler designs and efficiency strategies) illustrates flexible thinking.
weaknesses: While many ATL skills are described, there is limited explicit linkage between these skills and the resulting quality improvements in your product.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: You apply a range of ATL skills thoughtfullyâfrom communication and collaboration to self-management and critical thinkingâbacked by practical examples. To strengthen this section, explicitly explain how each skill improved aspects of your final product (for example, how precision in measurement directly enhanced fit or how feedback cycles elevated design quality).

strengths: Clear, personally meaningful learning goal linked to your gymnastics experience, providing strong motivation and context.
Inclusion of FIG regulations as a success criterion demonstrates awareness of official requirements.
Dualâtimeline approach (general monthly overview plus detailed task planner) effectively structures your workflow.
Well-focused product description that incorporates consumer input, showing thoughtful planning of design choices and functionality.
weaknesses: Success criteria are relevant but lack quantifiable indicators (e.g., exact budget thresholds, comfort tolerances) to ensure measurability.
Detailed task plan lists activities and completion status but does not specify required resources or sequencing dependencies.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your planning shows a strong connection to personal interest and external standards, and your timeline structures the work clearly. To refine, make each success criterion measurable by adding quantitative targets, and enhance the feasibility of your plan by detailing the resources needed for each task and mapping out their dependencies.

strengths: Thorough use of budget data, tracking actual expenses against targets to demonstrate strong cost management.
Integration of survey data with quantitative ratings provides solid, evidence-based client feedback.
Mature overall evaluation that identifies realistic areas for improvement alongside key successes.
Clear articulation of how the final product meets the learning goal and aligns with your global context of personal expression.
weaknesses: Evaluation tables are comprehensive but could be reorganized (e.g., separate tables) for greater clarity.
Survey results are not explicitly linked back to your predefined aesthetic and function criteria to guide targeted improvements.
The one-point rubric lacks fully developed âareas for developmentâ for each criterion.
Reflection on the product against original success criteria needs deeper, criterion-by-criterion evidence.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your reflection demonstrates strong self-assessment, supported by quantitative data and clear alignment with your learning goals. For deeper insight, reorganize your evaluation tables for readability, explicitly tie survey feedback to specific success criteria, expand the âgrowsâ column in your rubric, and systematically evaluate your product against each initial success criterion with concrete examples.

annotations.comment: You list relevant success criteria but should quantify key indicators (e.g., exact budget threshold, comfort tolerances) to ensure each criterion is measurable.
Great inclusion of FIG regulations as a success criterionâyou demonstrate awareness of official requirements early in planning.
Your dualâtimeline approach (general monthly and detailed task planner) effectively structures your workflow and keeps you on track.
Good use of budget data: you tracked actual expenses against targets and demonstrated strong cost management.
The detailed plan lists tasks and completion status but would benefit from specifying required resources and sequencing dependencies.
Your evaluation of function, aesthetics, legality, budget, and communication is thorough, but consider reorganizing into separate tables for clarity.
Your overall evaluation identifies key strengths and realistic areas for improvement, showing mature self-assessment.
Excellent integration of survey data to gather client feedback; the average rating adds quantitative support to your evaluation.
The survey context is clear, but link results explicitly back to your aesthetic and function criteria to inform targeted improvements.
Your expense table clearly itemizes costs and sources, supporting transparent reflection on budget performance.
You succinctly articulate how your final product meets your learning goal and aligns with your global context of personal expression.
Incorporating client feedback at the finishing stage exemplifies effective reflection and communication ATL skills.
Your early design iteration and client feedback cycles demonstrate strong ATL collaboration and communication skills.
Detailing of measurement taking and fabric cutting shows effective application of self-management and precision skills.
Your sewing and embellishment sequence is well documented, reflecting strong planning and organizational skills.
You establish a clear, personally meaningful learning goal linked to your gymnastics experience, providing strong context and motivation for your project.
Your product description is well-focused and includes consumer input, demonstrating effective planning of design choices and functionality.
Your research plan is comprehensive, linking specific questions to both sewing and design and identifying diverse, relevant sources.
You reflect thoughtfully on source evaluation using CARRDS, which strengthens the reliability of your research evidence.
Your reflection on learning the sewing machine shows effective ATL skill application, as you built in practice time and tested stitch types.
The one-point rubric is a good start, but it currently lacks complete âareas for developmentâ. Expand your âgrowsâ column for each criterion.
Your CARRDS evaluation table clearly demonstrates critical analysis of a professional tutorial, showing how you validated credibility and relevance.
You apply critical thinking to adapt your plan when you fell behind, brainstorming simpler designs and efficiency strategies.
You describe the impact on your learning, but to deepen reflection evaluate your product against each original success criterion with specific evidence.
